title: Codette v3.6: Institutional Temporal Analysis via TimeTravelLens and Multi-Layer Runtime Protection via AEGIS
description: Documents two major systems integrated into the Codette multi-perspective reasoning engine (v3.6): (1) TimeTravelLens — a formal framework computing preemption gap Π(s), closure score C(s) across a ClosureClass taxonomy (CLOSED/DRIFT/SUPPRESSED/INEXPRESSIBLE), rupture indicator ℛ(s), beacon indicator ℬ(s), and high preemption zone Z^H from either structured InstitutionalState objects or unstructured text via InstitutionalExtractor; and (2) AEGIS Protection Layers — six production Python implementations (Layer 2: Landlock+DACL filesystem isolation; Layer 3: TPM 2.0+Secure Boot integrity; Layer 4: ML-KEM-768 post-quantum cocoon sealing + ML-DSA-65 boot verification via liboqs, NIST FIPS 203/204; Layer 5: pre-emptive healing from real cocoon fields; Layer 6: CocoonV3 schema + word-overlap validation; metrics engine: SQLite-backed forge-cycle logging). The lens fires automatically on institutional queries, stores results in CocoonV3 memory artifacts with provenance, annotates AEGIS deontological weighting on high-preemption-zone detections, and surfaces metrics in a live UI dashboard. All implementations are open-source. 33 tests, 0 failures.
notes: TimeTravelLens is a new framework original to this project. AEGIS protection layers are original implementations. The project's Perspective Dispersion metric Υ is independent of and convergent with Camlin (arXiv:2505.01464); see docs/ATTRIBUTION_perspective_dispersion.md for full attribution detail. Layer 4 (PQC cocoon sealing) is now a real implementation using liboqs-python (Open Quantum Safe): ML-KEM-768 (NIST FIPS 203) for key encapsulation and ML-DSA-65 (NIST FIPS 204) for digital signatures. See Protection_Layer/aegis_layer4_complete.py.
